run.root_cause: The notebook-derived Bayesian prior + embedding probe pipeline is live; next steps are submission bundle parity and calibration.

experiment.title: Calibrated prior fusion v2
experiment.hypothesis: Work item workitem-0002-calibrated-prior-fusion-v2: Calibrated prior fusion v2

decision.root_cause: Calibrated prior fusion v2 (run-0002-0002) scored 0.6648 val ROC-AUC, a -0.0005 regression vs the leader run-0003-0001 (0.6652). The prior fusion component is near-random on train (0.487) and below raw probe on validation (0.662), confirming Bayesian prior calibration extracts no meaningful signal. The prior fusion branch is exhausted after two failed attempts (run-0003-0002 and run-0002-0002).
decision.why: The prior fusion post-processing lane has been tested twice with consistent negative results. Both negative vetoes at confidence 0.68 confirm this axis should be closed. The immediate priority is shipping the critic-approved raw-probe leader bundle (run-0003-0001 at 0.6652) to secure a submission slot. After that, plan should target high-value axes: probe-head architecture improvements (MLP regularization without prior fusion) and revised class-coverage strategies, since brute-force 52→75 expansion was flat. Collapsing to another calibration tweak would waste experiment capacity with no signal basis.
decision.next_title: Probe-head architecture and revised coverage strategy
decision.portfolio_intent: Close prior fusion (tertiary, exhausted). Ship primary data_coverage leader. Open probe-head architecture as next experimental axis.

problem_frame.focus: Ship the raw-probe leader submission bundle and open a probe-head architecture experiment axis. Prior fusion is exhausted.
problem_frame.root_cause: Prior fusion has been tested twice with negative results; the Bayesian prior adds no signal. The leader at 0.6652 is critic-approved and ready for submission. Next gains likely come from probe-head architecture rather than post-processing.

research.root_cause: Calibrated prior fusion v2 (run-0002-0002) achieved val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc=0.6648, a -0.0005 regression versus the current leader run-0003-0001 (0.6652). The prior fusion component itself is near-random on train (prior_fusion_macro_roc_auc=0.487) and only marginally above raw probe on validation (val_prior_fusion_macro_roc_auc=0.662), confirming that the Bayesian prior calibration has not extracted meaningful signal. The prior fusion post-processing lane should be deprioritized in favor of shipping the existing raw-probe leader bundle and pursuing class-coverage or probe-head improvements.
queries: run-0003-0001 class coverage leader submission bundle
perch probe head complementary signal embedding
adopt_now: [card:research-run-0003-0002-prior-fusion-weight-calibration-md-adopt-now] Ship run-0003-0001 (leader at 0.6652) or run-0002-0001 raw-probe submission bundle. Use raw probe output exclusively — prior fusion degrades it (0.6622). The bundle is already validated and critic-approved.

[card:01-validated-findings-md-perch-and-embedding-findings] Embedding-head style models can add complementary signal even when their standalone score is not the best (require @ 0.88). Keep the perch embedding probe as the primary signal source.
[memory:memory-0002] Primary data_coverage branch is the leader at 0.665216 with critic=approved. This is the submission candidate to bundle first.
consider: [card:03-next-experiment-priors-md-perch-probe-lane] If fitted classes lag active classes, expand coverage before micro-tuning. Coverage expansion from 52→75 classes was flat, so further coverage gains may require different class selection strategy rather than brute-force expansion.

[card:research-run-0002-0003-coverage-mlp-combined-full-75-class-mlp-probe-md-current-state] The combined coverage expansion + MLP regularization branch achieved 0.672 on a different metric context — worth investigating whether MLP regularization alone (without prior fusion) could lift the probe head.
[memory:memory-0001] Baseline run validated at 0.6651 with submission_status=candidate_created. This is a fallback submission if the leader bundle has packaging issues.
reject: [card:research-run-0003-0002-prior-fusion-weight-calibration-md-root-cause] Prior fusion weight calibration regresses by -0.003 vs leader. Do not allocate further submission slots or experiment iterations to prior fusion calibration until a fundamentally different prior source or calibration method is available.

The prior fusion component (val_prior_fusion_macro_roc_auc=0.662) is below the raw probe baseline, confirming it adds negative value as currently implemented. Any future prior fusion work must first demonstrate standalone lift on validation before being blended.
Padded_cmap remains very low (0.062) across all runs — segment-level localization is not the bottleneck for ROC-AUC but should not be a focus area until the primary metric plateau is broken.

branch_memories.summary: primary data_coverage -> leader; val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc=0.665216; delta=+0.000138; critic=approved; validate=not_required
branch_memories.root_cause: The notebook-derived Bayesian prior + embedding probe pipeline is live with expanded class coverage (52→75 active classes, 708 cache rows). The probe head recovered signal from 23 previously uncovered classes, but the gap between train soundscape ROC-AUC (0.992) and validation soundscape ROC-AUC (0.665) indicates overfitting or domain shift. Prior fusion validation metric (0.662) closely tracks the primary metric, suggesting the Bayesian prior adds marginal improvement at this stage. The padded_cmap remains very low (0.062), pointing to weak segment-level localization. Next steps are submission bundle parity and calibration.

branch_memories.summary: branch perch_cached_probe -> flat; val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc=0.665078; delta=-0.000138; critic=approved; validate=validated
branch_memories.root_cause: The notebook-derived Bayesian prior + embedding probe pipeline is live and functional, but submission bundle parity and calibration remain incomplete. The training soundscape ROC-AUC (0.992) is dramatically higher than the validation soundscape ROC-AUC (0.665), indicating potential overfitting or domain shift between training soundscapes and validation soundscapes. Prior fusion validation (0.662) closely tracks the raw validation score, suggesting the Bayesian prior is not yet adding significant lift.
